data.title: Trust Wallet Users Advised to Secure Assets Amid Security Concerns
data.description: According to Foresight News, SlowMist's Chief Information Security Officer, 23pds, has issued a warning to users affected by certain versions of Trust Wallet. He advised these users to disconnect from the internet before exporting their mnemonic phrases to transfer assets. This precaution is necessary to prevent asset theft when opening the wallet online. Users with mnemonic phrase backups are urged to transfer their assets before upgrading the wallet.

data.title: Trust Wallet Compromise: Binance to Reimburse $7M via SAFU
data.description:
Trust Wallet's extension vulnerability leads to $7 million in losses. Binance commits to reimbursing users using SAFU funds. Investigation ongoing.
source: https://coincu.com/news/trust-wallet-extension-vulnerability/

data.title: Ethereum unlikely to reach new highs in 2026: Ben Cowen
data.description:
If Ether manages to reclaim its all-time high in 2026, it may just be a “bull trap,” says crypto analyst Ben Cowen.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/ether-price-new-highs-2026-unlikely-ben-cowen-crypto-analyst?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Lithuania will begin cracking down on unlicensed encryption companies starting January 1, 2026.
data.description: The Central Bank of Lithuania, Lietuvos Bankas, has warned that from January 1, 2026, all institutions providing cryptocurrency-related services in the country must hold a MiCA license, or they will be considered operating illegally. The transition period ends on December 31, 2025. Non-compliant platforms may face fines, website bans, and in serious cases, criminal prosecution, with a maximum penalty of four years imprisonment. The regulator requires businesses that do not intend to continue operating to exit the market in an orderly manner and properly handle user assets. To date, of the more than 370 registered cryptocurrency companies in Lithuania, only about 30 have submitted license applications. (Cryptopolitan)

data.title: This week's top mining news highlights: Greenridge Capital gives Cango a "Buy" rating with a target price of $4.00; Bitcoin mining difficulty increased by 0.04% to 148.26T.
data.description: Week 52 of 2025 (December 13 - December 25): 1. According to Cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 1064 EH/s, with a high of 1171 EH/s and a low of 1017 EH/s, a 3.27% increase from the previous week's average hashrate (1030 EH/s). 2. According to Cloverpool, the mining difficulty was 148.26 T, the block height was 929,376, and the average block time was 10 minutes and 40 seconds, a 0.04% increase from the last adjustment. 3. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $87,718, with a high of $90,542 and a low of $85,053, a 1.22% decrease from the previous week's average price ($88,801). 4. Notable mining news includes: (1) Tether-related companies sold Northern Data’s mining business, and Rumble subsequently announced its acquisition of Northern Data; (2) The US SEC accused the founder of Bitcoin mining company VBit of misappropriating $48.5 million and committing fraud; (3) Greenridge Capital gave Cango a “buy” rating with a target price of $4.00; (4) “Bitcoin Rodney,” the initiator of the HyperFund crypto scam, was charged with additional wire fraud and could face decades in prison; (5) Hyperscale Data increased its Bitcoin holdings to 515 and allocated $30.5 million to continue purchasing; (6) Canaan Technology launched a $30 million stock buyback program. Data partner: NYSE-listed Bitcoin mining company Cango Inc. (CANG).

data.title: CZ: Trust Wallet will fully cover the $7 million loss caused by the hack.
data.description: Binance founder CZ stated on the X platform that the hack has resulted in a loss of $7 million, which TrustWallet will cover. User funds are safe, and the team is still investigating how the hackers submitted the new version.

data.title: Asia Market Open: Bitcoin Steady At $89k As Thin Trading Lifts Stocks, Silver Shines
data.description:
Bitcoin hovered near $87k in thin holiday trade as Asian stocks edged up, with analysts pointing to low liquidity keeping prices pinned despite a steady risk tone.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoin-price-thin-trading-lifts-stocks-silver-record-high-asia-market-open/

data.title: Trust Wallet Security Concerns Raised Over PostHog JS Script
data.description: According to PANews, SlowMist founder Yu Jin has raised concerns about a potential security issue involving Trust Wallet. Yu Jin stated that attackers appear to be well-acquainted with the Trust Wallet extension source code, having embedded PostHog JS to collect various user wallet information. Despite Trust Wallet releasing a fix, the PostHog JS script has not been removed.

data.title: SlowMist CISO: Trust Wallet-related developer devices or code repositories may be compromised by attackers.
data.description: According to 23pds, Chief Information Security Officer of SlowMist Technology, their analysis suggests that the devices or code repositories of Trust Wallet developers may have been compromised by attackers. Users are urged to disconnect from the internet and check their devices immediately. Previously, on-chain analyst Yu Xian published an article on the X platform stating that Trust Wallet browser extension version 2.68.0 contained a backdoor, which was fixed in version 2.69.0. Code comparison revealed that the backdoor added a PostHog plugin to collect wallet users' private information (including mnemonic phrases) and send it to the attacker's server api.metrics-trustwallet[.]com. Based on the estimated timeline, the attackers began preparations on December 8th, successfully implanted the backdoor on December 22nd, and began transferring funds on December 25th. According to zachxbt, user losses have exceeded $6 million.

data.title: Analyst Predicts Surge in Prediction Market Trading Volume
data.description: According to Odaily, analyst Patrick Scott shared on the X platform that last month's prediction market trading volume exceeded $13 billion, which is more than triple the volume expected during the peak of the 2024 election. The majority of this trading activity was dominated by platforms such as Polymarket, Kalshi, and OPINION. Binary options have now been applied to areas like political speeches, sports events, and corporate earnings reports, becoming a layer of probability for global events and news.

data.title: Bitcoin's Short-Term Outlook Remains Uncertain Amid Potential ETF Outflows
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Chinese crypto analyst Ban Muxia shared insights on social media regarding Bitcoin's market outlook. The analyst suggested that while it is no longer appropriate to be overly bullish on Bitcoin, there is no need to be excessively bearish over the next one to two months. However, adjustments may occur around March or April next year. Ban Muxia noted that a significant outflow of funds from ETFs could lead to a sharp decline, potentially causing Bitcoin to fall below $80,500 or even to $71,000, though this is considered a low-probability event. Currently, Bitcoin's volatility has narrowed significantly, indicating that a directional move may occur soon.

data.title: Aave's Proposal to Transfer Brand Assets to DAO Rejected
data.description: According to PANews, a proposal on the Aave governance forum to transfer Aave's brand assets, including domain names, social media accounts, and naming rights, to DAO control was rejected on December 26. The proposal, known as ARFC, aimed to address concerns over third-party control of these assets and sought to clarify ownership and usage through a DAO legal structure. The vote concluded with 994,800 votes against the proposal, representing 55.29% of the total, while only 63,000 votes were in favor. Additionally, 41.21% of participants abstained from voting.

data.title: Data: Last month's forecast market trading volume exceeded $13 billion, setting a new record.
data.description: Analyst Patrick Scott wrote on the X platform that the prediction market saw trading volume exceeding $13 billion last month, more than three times the peak trading volume during the 2024 election. Polymarket, Kalshi, and OPINION accounted for the vast majority of the trading volume. Binary options are now used in areas such as political speeches, sporting events, and corporate financial reports, becoming a probability layer for world events and news.

data.title: PeckShield: The TrustWallet hack has resulted in the theft of over $6 million, of which over $4 million has been transferred to exchanges.
data.description: PeckShieldAlert stated on the X platform that the TrustWallet vulnerability exploit has resulted in victims losing over $6 million worth of cryptocurrency. Currently, approximately $2.8 million of the stolen funds remain in the hacker's wallet (involving Bitcoin, EVM, and Solana), while the majority of the funds (over $4 million) have been sent to centralized exchanges: approximately $3.3 million to ChangeNOW, approximately $340,000 to FixedFloat, and approximately $447,000 to kucoincom.
